messages.author: PiecesAndPawns
messages.text: I'd be concerned with the line snapping, or pulling the fletching off. An arrow shot from any decent bow is going to have some velocity/inertia. Arrows that are shot skyward can be deadly.. even if not tipped with a hunting tip.
messages.content_html: I'd be concerned with the line snapping, or pulling the fletching off. An arrow shot from any decent bow is going to have some velocity/inertia. Arrows that are shot skyward can be deadly.. even if not tipped with a hunting tip.

messages.author: Orum
messages.text: After reading through my state's statues makes it very clear that a bow should not be used for this.
messages.content_html: After reading through my state's statues makes it very clear that a bow should not be used for this.

messages.author: Orum
messages.text: Nevermind, slingshots are in fact forbidden by my municipality: No person shall sell, or offer for sale and no person shall purchase, carry, go armed with or shoot or use any slingshot or similar device designed to throw or propel any missile
messages.content_html:

messages.author: PiecesAndPawns
messages.text: depending on th hardware.. even significant shading may still work. I had a wisblock in a tree with a 6w panel.. shaded most of the day, and still produced more than enough power to keep the battery charged. I've seen a 6w panel power awisblock on without a battery.. in a full moon. (I obviously wouldn't recommend it cuz you'll eventually corrupt the filesystem, but.. thats how little power it uses.
messages.content_html: depending on th hardware.. even significant shading may still work. I had a wisblock in a tree with a 6w panel.. shaded most of the day, and still produced more than enough power to keep the battery charged. I've seen a 6w panel power awisblock on without a battery.. in a full moon. (I obviously wouldn't recommend it cuz you'll eventually corrupt the filesystem, but.. thats how little power it uses.

messages.author: Orum
messages.text: Yeah, it really depends if you're using nRF vs ESP32. The latter takes ~20x the power. (edited)
messages.content_html: Yeah, it really depends if you're using nRF vs ESP32. The latter takes ~20x the power. (edited)
